title: CHIEF MINISTER MEETS UNION MINISTER
content:
91/2026-2027

Aizawl, 17th May 2026 :Chief Minister Lalduhoma today met Smt Nimuben Jayantibhai Bambhaniya, Union Minister of State for Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution, who is currently on a visit to Mizoram, at his official residence in Aizawl at 2:00 PM.

The Chief Minister welcomed the Union Minister to Mizoram and conveyed his best wishes for a pleasant stay in the state. During the meeting, he raised four important matters concerning Mizoram :

Request for enhanced allocation of rice under the Open Market Sales Scheme (OMSS).

Request for permission to procure rice through Bharat Rice in cases where procurement under OMSS is not feasible.

Request for enhancement of Mizoram’s rice allocation quota under the National Food Security Act (NFSA).

Fund from Central Sector Scheme of 'Storage & Godown' for the construction of three godowns in Mizoram.

The Union Minister assured the Chief Minister that the matters raised would be duly pursued.

She also expressed her appreciation for the scenic beauty and hospitality of Mizoram.

The Chief Minister was accompanied by Pu B. Lalchhanzova and senior officials from FCS & CA.

Attachment

title: CHIEF MINISTER LEH UNION MINISTER INKAWM
content:
570/2026-2027

Aizawl the 17th May 2026 :Vawiin dar 2 khan Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma chuan a Bangla-ah Mizoram tlawha lo zin mek Pi Nimuben Jayantibhai Bambhaniya, Union Minister of State for Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution chu a kawm.

Chief Minister hian Mizoram tlawha a rawn zin avangin lawmthu a hrilh a, hlim taka hmun hmang tura duhsakna hlanin Mizoram tana thil pawimawh pali a sawipui a, chungte chu:

1. Open Market Sales Scheme (OMSS) hnuaia buhfai tam zawk lei phalsak chungchang.

2. OMSS atanga lei a rem loh pawha Bharat Rice atanga lei phalsak.

3. NFSA Mizoram rice allocation Quota tihpun chungchang, leh

4. Central sector scheme awm thin, 'Storage & Godown' tihnun thar leh chungchangte an ni.

Union Minister hian Chief Minister thusawite chu a lo bawhzui tur thu a sawi a. Mizoram chu mawi a tih thu leh nuam a tih thu Chief Minister hnenah hian a sawi bawk.

Chief Minister hi Pu B. Lalchhanzova, FCS & CA Minister leh official pawimawhten an tawiawm a ni.

Attachment

title: FCS&CA Department Hosts Interaction-cum-Dinner Programme in honour of Union Minister of State Smt. Nimuben Jayantibhai Bambhaniya
content:
90/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 17th May, 2026 :Last night, the Food, Civil Supplies & Consumer Affairs (FCS&CA) Department, Government of Mizoram hosted an Interaction-cum-Dinner Programme in honour of the visiting Union Minister of State (MoS) for Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution (CAF&PD), Smt. Nimuben Jayantibhai Bambhaniya, at the R.B. McCabe Hall, Aijal Club, Aizawl. The event was attended by FCS&CA Minister Shri B. Lalchhanzova; FCS&CA Secretary Shri Ravi Dadhich, IAS; PS to MoS Smt. Jagriti Rohit Singla, IRTS; Addl PS to MoS Smt. Khushbu Suthar, GAS; Food Corporation of India (FCI) General Manager (NEF Region) Shri Sandeep Deora, along with other FCI and state officials.

Addressing the gathering, Minister of State Smt. Nimuben Jayantibhai Bambhaniya expressed her happiness at being able to visit Mizoram. She remarked that Mizoram is home to many unique indigenous products and vibrant cultural heritage, such as the GI-tagged Mizo Ginger and Mizo Chilli, besides the world-famous Cheraw dance. However, she stated that her visit was not merely to admire the beauty and achievements of the State, but also to understand the challenges faced by Mizoram first-hand and extend necessary support.

Smt. Nimuben highlighted that under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modiji, around 81 crore people continue to receive free ration across the country. She pointed out that this population is larger than that of entire Europe, making it the largest food security programme in the world. Attributing this to the Prime Minister’s high regard for 'Food for All', the MoS assured that they would make every possible effort to ensure that everyone in Mizoram continues to have sufficient and quality food to eat.The Minister appreciated the initiatives and achievements of the FCS&CA Department, stating that systems like e-KYC, EPOS and SMART PDS have greatly benefitted the people. Furthermore, acknowledging that heavy monsoon rains often disrupt road connectivity in the Northeast, Smt. Nimuben expressed her willingness to assist northeastern states by ensuring advance allocation of ration for up to three months before the onset of the monsoon. She also offered to convey Mizoram's grievances to the Union CAF&PD Minister Shri Pralhad Joshi and arrange a meeting with him for further discussion.

Speaking on the occasion, FCS&CA Minister Pu B. Lalchhanzova thanked the MoS for visiting Mizoram and personally assessing the various challenges faced by the State. He also extended his gratitude to the Central Government and the FCI for their continued support in ensuring a steady food supply for Mizoram. The Minister further stated that the Mizoram Government would continue to strengthen cooperation with the Central Government to ensure that no citizen is deprived of their rightful entitlements. He also requested the MoS to consider enhancement of Mizoram’s NFSA quota to further strengthen food supply and ensure “Food for All” in the State.

FCS&CA Department Secretary Shri Ravi Dadhich also expressed gratitude to the MoS for visiting Mizoram and personally reviewing the ground realities of the State. Through a PowerPoint presentation, he highlighted the current status and initiatives of the Department, and explained the ongoing State Government programmes, namely the Non-NFSA and MSSRP schemes.

Subsequently, FCI General Manager (NEF Region) Shri Sandeep Deora also made a PowerPoint presentation on various initiatives undertaken by FCI for Mizoram in the areas of food supply and warehousing, their current status, the challenges being faced by them, and the measures required to overcome them.

Last night's programme was chaired by FCS&CA Department Director Smt. Saizikpuii, who delivered the welcome address and also concluded the programme with a vote of thanks. This was followed by an informal interaction session and dinner for all the attendees.

Attachment

title: FCS&CA Department-in Union Minister of State Pi Nimuben Jayantibhai Bambhaniya pualin Inkawmhona leh Zanriah kilhona hun an nei
content:
569/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 17th May, 2026 :Nizan khan Food, Civil Supplies & Consumer Affairs (FCS&CA) Department, Government of Mizoram chuan Mizoram tlawha lo zin mek Union Minister of State(MoS) for Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution(CAF&PD) Pi Nimuben Jayantibhai Bambhaniya pualin Aijal Club-a R.B. McCabe Hall-ah Inkawmhona leh Zanriah kilhona hun(Interaction-cum-Dinner Programme) a buatsaih a. He hunah hian FCS&CA Minister Pu B. Lalchhanzova; FCS&CA Secretary Pu Ravi Dadhich, IAS; PS to MoS Pi Jagriti Rohit Singla, IRTS; Addl PS to MoS Pi Khushbu Suthar, GAS; Food Corporation of India (FCI) General Manager (NEF Region) Pu Sandeep Deora te bakah FCI leh state official dangte an tel a ni.

Minister of State Pi Nimuben Jayantibhai Bambhaniya chuan Mizoram a lo kal thei hi a lawm thu sawiin, Mizoram chuan GI tag nei tawh Mizo Sawhthing leh Mizo Hmarcha te mai bakah khawvel hriat khawpa hmingthang Cheraw lam ang hian thil danglam leh tualchhuak thil engemaw zat a neih thu a sawi a; amaherawhchu, Pi Nimuben chuan Mizoram a tlawh chhan chu a mawina leh hlawhtlinnate thlir ringawt tur ni loin state-in harsatna a neihte zira, a tul anga chingfel tura a lo kal thu a sawi a ni.

Pi Nimuben chuan Prime Minister Narendra Modi hruaina hnuaiah hian mi vaibelchhia 81 (81 crore)-in a thlawnin ration an dawng reng a, hei hi Europe rama mihring awm zat aia tam a nih bakah khawvel puma ei leh bar khamkhawp inngaihtuahsakna (food security program) lian ber a ni, a ti. Hei hi PM-in 'Mi zawng zawng tan Chaw' (Food for All) a ngaih pawimawh vang a nih thu sawiin, MoS chuan Mizoramah pawh mi tinin eikhawp hnianghnar an neih reng theih nan theihtawp an chhuah dawn tih a tiam a ni. MoS chuan FCS&CA Department hmalakna leh hlawhtlinnate fakin, e-KYC, EPOS leh SMART PDS te hian mi tam tak a tanpui a ni, a ti a. Hemi bakah hian MoS chuan hmarchhak bialah fur ruahtui tlak nasat avanga inkalpawhna kawngpui a chhiat fo thin thu sawiin hmarchhak state-te hnenah fur thlen hmain thla 3 daih ration pek lawk an nih theih nan tanpui a inhuam thu a sawi bawk a. Chutih rualin, Mizoram harsatnate CAF&PD Minister Pu Pralhad Joshi hnen thlenpui a, inkawmna hun siamsak a inhuam thu a sawi bawk a ni.

He hunah hian Minister Pu B. Lalchhanzova pawhin thu sawiin Mizoram tlawh a, state harsatna hrang hrangte a hmun ngeia a rawn zirchian avangin MoS Pi Nimuben hnenah lawmthu a sawi a. Mizoram eikhawp (food supply) a awm reng theih nana an tanpuina avangin Sorkar laipui leh FCI-te hnenah lawmthu a sawi bawk. Minister chuan Mizoram pawhin sorkar laipui nena thawhdunna tha tak neih zawm zelin, mi tinte chanvo tur chu a bothlau a awm lohnan tan nasa leh zuala an lak tur thu a sawi a. Mizorama food supply tihhmasawn zel nan leh 'Mi zawng zawng tan Chaw khawpkham (Food for All)' a thlen ngei theih nan Mizoram NFSA quota tihpun sak turin MoS chu a ngen bawk a ni.

FCS&CA Secretary Pu Ravi Dadhich pawhin Mizoram dinhmun dik tak a hmun ngeia a rawn thlir pui avangin MoS hnenah lawmthu a sawi a. PowerPoint presentation hmangin an department dinhmun leh hmalakna te tarlangin, state sawrkar kalpui mek- Non-NFSA(Card var) leh MSSRP(Card Sen) programme-te a hrilhfiah bawk a ni.

Nizan hun hmannaah hian FCI General Manager (NEF Region) Pu Sandeep Deora pawhin PowerPoint Presentation hmangin Mizoram tana food supply leh warehousing kawnga an hmalakna hrang hrang te, tun thlenga an dinhmun te, harsatna an tawh mekte leh chung harsatna sutkian nana hmalakna turte a tarlang bawk a ni.

Nizan programme hi FCS&CA Department Director Pi Saizikpuiin kaihruaiin, kalkhawmte lawmna thu a sawi a; lawmthu sawiin programme hi a khar bawk a ni. Hemi hnuah hian kalkhawmte pualin zanriah kilhona leh inkawmhona hun buatsaih zui a ni.

Attachment
